items.briefing:
Australian accounts payable teams are being warned about increasing fraud risk as AI makes it easier for scammers to impersonate suppliers and manipulate payment processes. Separately, a new survey highlights that while Australian healthcare organisations are spending more on cybersecurity, many lack confidence in their ability to recover from an attack — a concern given the sector's sensitive data.

There is also growing discussion around a "digital duty of care," pushing organisations to take greater accountability for how AI tools are used and the risks they introduce.

**Tip:** If you receive an unexpected request to change a supplier's bank details, always verify it by calling a known contact number — not one provided in the request itself.

featured.title: Mackay Sugar cyber attack flagged as broader risk to Australia’s food supply chain
featured.quote: Major Australian food exporter attacked, exposing critical infrastructure vulnerability in essential supply chain.
